articles.title: Arizona State Retirement System Decreases Position in The Walt Disney Company $DIS
articles.summary: The Arizona State Retirement System reduced its holdings in The Walt Disney Company by 3.4% during Q4, selling 17,868 shares to own 507,136 shares worth $57.7 million. Despite this decrease, institutional investors collectively own 65.71% of Disney's stock. The article also touches upon positive and negative sentiments surrounding Disney, analyst ratings, and financial performance data.

articles.title: Disney
articles.summary: This ABC News article compiles recent news and updates related to Disney, covering various entertainment releases, park information, and celebrity news. It highlights upcoming projects such as "Princess Diaries 3" and the "Toy Story 5" cereal tie-in, alongside recent events like Bob Iger's Disney Legend announcement and the "Hannah Montana 20th Anniversary Special." The content primarily focuses on Disney-affiliated media, films, and theme park developments.

articles.title: Kellogg is putting toys back into some cereal boxes as a 'Toy Story 5' tie-in
articles.summary: WK Kellogg Co. is reintroducing plastic toys into select cereal boxes, including Frosted Flakes, Froot Loops, AppleJacks, and Corn Pops, as a tie-in with Disney and Pixar's upcoming "Toy Story 5" movie. This marks the first time in over a decade that the company has included toys, aiming to bring back a sense of screen-free discovery for children and parents. The decision comes after toys largely disappeared from cereal boxes due to cost-cutting, safety concerns, and past recalls.

articles.title: How the ‘Hannah Montana 20th Anniversary Special’ Was Produced by Fans for Fans
articles.summary: The "Hannah Montana 20th Anniversary Special" was largely fan-driven, with Miley Cyrus manifesting the event and many Walt Disney Company employees who are long-time fans contributing to its production and promotion. The special saw significant success, garnering 6.3 million views in three days and a near 1,000% increase in views for the entire Hannah Montana catalog on Disney+. This fan-first approach highlighted the enduring popularity of the franchise and created a nostalgic "homecoming" for both Cyrus and fans involved in its creation.
